chapter_context.summary: Introduces the first Servant Song, describing the gentle Servant who brings justice to the Gentiles, followed by a new song of praise and rebuking blind servants.

outline.text: First Servant Song: Gentle Servant, Light to Gentiles
outline.explanation: This section focuses on First Servant Song: Gentle Servant, Light to Gentiles (42:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: New Song of Universal Praise to Lord Who Fights as Warrior
outline.explanation: This section focuses on New Song of Universal Praise to Lord Who Fights as Warrior (42:10-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Rebuking Blind and Deaf Servants Who Disobeyed Law
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Blind and Deaf Servants Who Disobeyed Law (42:18-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Comfort and Redemption
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of comfort and redemption, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 42, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
